message:
fix: make nightly E2E benchmark tests resilient to endpoint failures (#3692) (#3741)

The three nightly E2E tests in benchmark-dashboard.spec.ts were calling
response.json() directly, which crashes with an unhelpful error when the
Netlify function returns non-JSON (e.g. HTML error page during outages).

Extract a shared fetchNightlyData() helper that:
- Catches fetch errors (network timeouts, DNS failures)
- Handles non-OK HTTP status codes gracefully
- Parses JSON defensively with try/catch
- Returns null on any failure, letting tests call test.skip()

This prevents transient Netlify outages from causing hard test failures
in the nightly CI pipeline.

Co-authored-by: andy anderson <andy@andys-MacBook-Pro.local>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
